learning_outcomes.description: Evaluate and design performance management systems that align fundamental management theory, organisational goals, and ethical principles.
Evaluate the impact of performance management on employee motivation, engagement, and organisational performance.
Develop strategies for optimising performance management in organisations, integrating technology, data analytics, and best practices.
Demonstrate effective collaboration with peers through constructive feedback and coaching to enhance performance.

assessments.assessment_title: Evaluate and Design a Performance Management System - PROPOSAL
assessments.task_description:
Team Formation: At the start of the semester, you will be given the opportunity to form your team. Teams are student-selected and you will have the option to choose your peers and who you want to work with. The Lecturer encourages diversity in teams because research generally showed that diverse teams are more creative and productive. The composition of teams will remain constant throughout the semester, promoting stable group dynamics and consistent collaboration.

Task Description:

For this assessment, you will present an evaluation and design of a Performance Management System. This is a group project where you'll create and submit an In-Class In-Person Presentation, not to exceed 10 minutes in duration. In this presentation, your group will use concepts from the entire course to evaluate and design a performance management system within an organization that aligns fundamental management theory, organisational goals, and ethical principles. You must also evaluate the impact of this performance management system on employee motivation, engagement, and organizational performance. Your designed system should optimize performance management while integrating technology, data analytics, and best practices. You are also encouraged to include any other relevant topics you deem essential to make a persuasive case.

This task emphasizes collaborative effort and it is crucial that all team members contribute meaningfully to the presentation. Your teamwork on this assessment will contribute 30% to your semester grade. This presentation is not just about presenting facts; it's about working as a team to effectively communicate a comprehensive and convincing argument.

Additional Clarifications:

1. Scope and Objective:

  - The evaluation and design of the Performance Management System is not limited to a specific type of organization; it can be corporate, non-profit, government, etc.

  - Through analysis of the performance management system, teams should identify which concepts from the course to emphasize, based on the time available for the presentation.

2. Presentation Details:

*** All team members are required to present during the In-Class, In-Person team presentations.

- Student teams can determine the best format for their presentations (e.g., PowerPoint slides, live demonstration, animated video).

  - The presentation should be entirely focused on a real-life example or case study. It should not be designed on a fictional account of an organization or a completely fictional/made-up performance management system.

-Teams are encouraged to seek permission from the Lecturer for their choice of organization to evaluate in this assessment.

3. Group Collaboration:

  - The peer evaluation process will be established by students during the course using course concepts.

  - Expectations for the "meaningful contribution" of each team member will be determined by students during the semester using course concepts.

4. Evaluation Criteria:

  - A separate grading rubric will be used to assess the quality of the presentation. Students should refer to this rubric when producing their presentation.

  - All Teams' presentations should be submitted via Blackboard.

Teamwork and Collaboration: Success in team presentations requires not only a thorough understanding of the course content but also effective communication skills. You will need to discuss presentation ideas with your team members, apply course concepts in real-time, and convince your peers of the correct concepts/arguments, mirroring real-world professional scenarios.

Conflict Resolution: In the event of disagreements during team presentation or preparation for the presentations, groups will need to employ negotiation strategies discussed in the course. Leveraging HR concepts to manage group dynamics effectively is an integral part of the learning process and will enhance your practical understanding of the course material.

AI Statement

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person and in the class during lecture times.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Team presentations will be submitted via BlackBoard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Evaluate and Design a Performance Management System - REPORT
assessments.task_description:
Rationale

This assessment continues the process of performance management interventions and decision making and the use and application of the frameworks/Concepts from assessment 1.

The main difference between assessment task 1 and assessment task 2 is that, whereas students were asked to evaluate performance management systems in assessment 1 and present a team's proposal, students will have the opportunity to identify and select a contemporary organisation or business in Australia or overseas for assessment 2 to apply their performance management frameworks/concepts to performance management issues affecting their chosen organisation. Students are expected to critically analyse the performance management issues facing their chosen organisation and write a business report with solutions and recommendations that constructively address the performance management issues in their chosen organisation. In other words, Assessment 1 focuses on a team's proposal of an organisation or business performance management system while Assessment 2 focuses on a team's report of an organisation or business performance management system. 

Note: Student teams may choose different performance management frameworks/concepts (from the frameworks used in assessment 1) if the student teams think other relevant frameworks/concepts are more appropriate and necessary to address the performance management issues in the student team's chosen organisation or business.

Working as team in an organisation or business, your team is expected to present your solutions and recommendations in a business report. The report will be submitted on blackboard.

Purpose

The aim of assessment task 2 is to enable students to build on their knowledge and skills of performance management theories, principles and processes through a case study method. Student teams will also understand the connections between theory and practice and develop practical skills in analysing, designing, implementing and evaluating performance management processes and practices.

Tasks

Your team will produce a 2,000-word business report with at least 10 academic peer-reviewed sources. Your team's report will address the following:

  1. Decide on a medium to large organisations where there is a performance management challenge/issue. Also, it is NOT necessary to contact the organisation for interviews or visits. Student teams should rely only on secondary research including but not limited to company websites, strategic plans, annual reports, industry and analysts' reports.
  2. Based on the contents of MGTS7605 seminar activities, your team will determine the major performance management challenges/issues the organisation is facing and why it is important to study and resolve this issue in the organisation.
  3. Conduct a review of the literature on the performance management issue and identify major schools of thought about the performance management issues, theories/models that best explain the performance management issues and appropriate interventions to address the issues. Preferably, it is important to begin with the theories and models discussed in the course. However, your team is NOT limited to the theories and models discussed in the course. 
  4. Critically examine the performance management issues in your team's case organisation using theories and models from the course as well as your team's literature review materials.
  5. Provide evidence of clear and concise articulation of the connection between performance management theories/models and practice.
  6. Develop and present a clear set of recommendations to the executive director, CEO or Human Resource Manager of your team's case organisation on how to implement your proposed recommendations.
  7. Identify two key lessons your team learned in writing and reporting on performance management system issues using a case study method.

The assignment is to be submitted as follows

  • The font will be 12 pt Times New Roman/Arial.
  • Line spacing will be 1 ½ (1.5).
  • Word count and limit (2,000 words +/- 10%)
  • Paragraphs will have first sentence indentation.
  • The report should have
  • an executive summary,
  • introduction
  • body of the report
  • conclusion
  • appendices
  • a minimum of 10 references
  • Appendices may be used as supporting material. Appendices must be:
  • (1) referenced in the text, and
  • (2) kept in order of their reference.

Note

Support and further information about assessment 2 is available on Blackboard.

Criteria & Marking:

Responses will be evaluated based on evidence of student teams’ ability to: (a) analyse a real-world problem, (b) apply relevant performance management principles to that problem, (c) formulate concrete strategies for action, (d) justify those recommendations, and (e) communicate them effectively and efficiently. Marking will also reflect the overall quality of the team's recommendation(s), the practicality of their proposed changes, and consistency with general principles of best practice. Each prompt will indicate the number of marks possible for that question.

Submission:

Your team should submit your assessment via Turnitin on Blackboard on or before the due date.

Complex/authentic assessment using Artificial Intelligence to Support Learning

Artificial Intelligence (AI) provides emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Student teams may appropriately use AI in completing this assessment task. Student teams must clearly reference any use of AI in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The project must be submitted electronically via a Turn-It-In link in the Assessment section of BlackBoard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The final exam in this course will cover all topics; that includes all material presented in the seminars (by the instructor and by guest speakers), in readings and in class teams' presentations. The exam tests both your understanding of the theoretical knowledge and its application. The exam will be a combination of multiple choice and written short questions.

AI Statement

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
